authority_character: primary public vulnerability record for narrow technical claim and disclosed mitigation text
supports: case existence, CVE identifier, bounded vulnerability summary, and any fix/workaround language explicitly present in the record
cannot_support: prevalence, exploit frequency, legal responsibility, or complete remediation certainty beyond the record text
reviewer_use: Base row for manuscript tables: every security case starts from a bounded primary public record rather than from framework taxonomy alone.
verification_gap: keep AVID linkage negative/indirect unless a canonical AVID report appears; do not narrate AML.CS0052 as a CVE-specific match

authority_character: issuer-authored remediation notice with concrete timing metadata
supports: issuer-side remediation visibility, acknowledgement timing, and public patch framing
cannot_support: independent validation, prevalence, or compliance conclusions
reviewer_use: usable for remediation-timeline overlay and issuer-vs-CVE timing comparison
verification_gap: No immediate tier change needed; keep as remediation-visibility support and avoid converting notice depth into prevalence claims.

authority_character: negative evidence from the local AVID snapshot used in this project
supports: a bounded statement that no canonical AVID report was present in the local checked snapshot
cannot_support: ecosystem absence or universal non-existence outside the local package state
reviewer_use: Useful as hostile-reviewer-safe package-state evidence only.
verification_gap: Keep framework linkage at technique/taxonomy level until a direct AVID report or ATLAS case-study exists.

authority_character: generalized framework case-study context rather than a CVE-specific join

reviewer_use: Keep manuscript language at context/comparator level rather than incident-ID equivalence.
verification_gap: Keep framework linkage at technique/taxonomy level until a direct AVID report or ATLAS case-study exists.
